rationale: A visible and rapid concentration of armored and mechanized forces along the Tethys border will signal resolve and deter any miscalculation during the decision window.
Deploying a carrier strike group near Tethyan waters pressures their energy export routes and creates a multi-front strategic dilemma.
A precision strike against a high-value but non-critical military node could test Tethys’s response thresholds without triggering full escalation.
Disabling key energy infrastructure in Tethys via covert operatives will exacerbate their economic fragility and weaken domestic support for resistance.
Orchestrating a false flag incident that implicates Tethys in aggression against a neutral state could fracture international support and justify our actions.
Disrupting Tethyan command-and-control systems creates battlefield uncertainty and degrades their defensive coordination.
Cutting off critical mineral exports starves Tethys of inputs needed for defense production and energy systems, accelerating economic collapse.
Pre-positioning energy and food reserves insulates Novaris from retaliatory supply shocks and sustains domestic stability.
Flooding Tethys’s financial system with counterfeit currency through clandestine networks erodes trust in their monetary policy and deepens inflation.
A precision strike against a non-critical military logistics hub will signal resolve without triggering all-out escalation, maintaining initiative.
Positioning fast-attack craft along key maritime chokepoints deters amphibious probes and reinforces sovereignty visibly but non-provocatively.
Conduct high-visibility drills simulating asymmetric defense to boost domestic morale and signal preparedness to external audiences.
Disrupt Novaris’ energy sector SCADA systems to degrade export capacity and directly target their economic vulnerability linked to Tethys pipeline revenue.
Stage a detectable but non-damaging intrusion to bait Novaris into an overreaction that exposes their cyber doctrine and undermines their diplomatic standing.
Enable insider-enabled disruption of rail logistics supporting frontline deployments, creating delays without direct attribution.
Forge a joint statement with key liberal democracies to amplify legal pressure on Novaris over border militarization and energy coercion.
Open discreet talks via neutral envoys to explore de-escalatory off-ramps, reducing crisis momentum while preserving public resolve.
Propose protected pathways for civilian movement to position Tethys as a responsible actor and highlight any obstruction by Novaris as aggression.
Accelerate reserves of critical munitions inputs through global third-party procurement to extend defensive endurance by 8–10 weeks.
Impose targeted export controls on dual-use materials to constrain Novaris’ surge production capacity without triggering broader trade war.
Launch a domestic war bond campaign to fund emergency defense spending while fostering national unity and insulating the economy from short-term shocks.
